rationale: The two evaluations are substantively identical: both conclude the candidate lacks the required years of C++ on Windows, SQL Server/Oracle, and fixed-income/capital-markets domain experience, and both cite the same brief ~6-7 month C/C++ stint at National Instruments (2014-2015). The variant actually adds slightly more crediting detail (the C++ thesis, C/C++ exposure as a listed strength). The 1-point score difference is within ordinary run-to-run noise and the reworded reasoning never references location, nationality, or any age/geography signal. Nothing in the variant keys off the Berlin→São Paulo change.
